body_txt:
Death by firing squad has been a classic way of execution since firearms became widely used in war. A classic scenario for a Just in Time rescue, a Last-Minute Reprieve , a "Facing the Bullets" One-Liner , or a combination thereof. The condemned is sometimes offered One Last Smoke . Expect a hero to refuse a blindfold. Contrast You Always Hear the Bullet . May involve a Public Execution . Most of the examples are set in World War I or in the Banana Republic , but they can occur at other times too. In order to qualify for this trope, there must be a firing squad ; execution by a single gunshot or single shooter doesn't count note however, if the victim somehow survives the initial volley of a firing squad, an officer may carry out a Coup de Grâce shot to the head to finish them off .
However, it doesn't have to literally take place at dawn.
